steps.title: Identify the link between the prize criterion and Synge's work
steps.content: The passage establishes that the Nobel Prize honors “the greatest benefit to mankind.” Since Synge won the prize, his work must represent a positive contribution to chemistry.

steps.title: Apply the criterion to find the match
steps.content: ”Advancement in” means progress or improvement—directly aligning with the “greatest benefit” criterion for the Nobel Prize. The other three either contradict this criterion (controversy is negative), create grammatical awkwardness (exposure to), or misrepresent the nature of scientific contribution (dependence on). That matches choice D — D is correct.
